title: PEM Certificate
documentation:
## Purpose
PEM (Privacy Enhanced Mail) is a Base64-encoded text encoding of DER-format ASN.1 structures, delimited by `-----BEGIN ...-----` / `-----END ...-----` headers. The [PEM Certificate](https://capdag.com/browse/media:pem;textable) media type represents files containing X.509 certificates, private keys, certificate chains, or certificate signing requests in PEM encoding.

## When to use
Use this type when a node produces or consumes TLS certificates, private keys, or certificate signing requests — for example, TLS provisioning pipelines, CA automation, secret management workflows, or mTLS configuration. PEM is the standard format used by OpenSSL, nginx, Apache, and most ACME clients.

## Notes
A single PEM file may contain multiple concatenated PEM objects (e.g., a leaf certificate followed by intermediate certificates). The `;textable` tag reflects that PEM is ASCII-safe. Implementers should treat PEM files as sensitive artifacts containing secrets; private key files should not be logged or stored without encryption. Use OpenSSL or a cryptography library to parse and validate PEM content.
